name: Background Task Intent
summary: Protocol defining the persistent surface that hosts long-running work after it goes async — the home a Loader hands a task to past its escalation threshold. Covers task registration that outlives the originating view, aggregation of concurrent tasks with per-task progress and retry, and the navigation-away / before-unload guard. Distinct from the Feedback Intent (transient toasts) and the Loader Intent (in-view pending state).

aggregation.description: How concurrent backgrounded tasks are presented in the surface

persistence.description: How long a completed task's entry remains in the surface

navigationGuard.description: Whether an in-flight task warns the user before they navigate away or close

description:
What This Is

The Loader Intent's escalation: async value says that past a longer platform-defined duration a still-running task should go async: a persistent status surface stays present, the user is free to navigate away, and a window prompt warns before they close or leave (the task continues in the background). The Loader declares that this should happen; the Background Task Intent defines the surface that hosts those backgrounded tasks — the receiving end of that handoff.

It is a distinct UX surface, not a loader. It outlives the originating view, aggregates multiple concurrent tasks, shows per-task progress, and mediates the navigation-away warning. It is not the Feedback Intent (transient, fire-and-forget toasts) and not the Loader Intent (in-view pending state that dies with its view).

Platform anchor: the browser's Background Fetch API models exactly this shape — long transfers that survive navigation, with browser-provided progress UI — but this intent's scope is broader than fetches (any long task), so it borrows the vocabulary without requiring a service worker.

Aggregation

How concurrent backgrounded tasks present in the surface.

  • single: One task at a time. The surface shows a single progress entry.
  • batch: Multiple concurrent tasks, each with its own progress and retry — one task's failure does not abort the others (unless they are dependent). Mirrors the Loader Intent's determinate guidance for parallel uploads.

Persistence

How long a completed task's entry remains in the surface.

  • transient: The entry auto-clears once the task resolves successfully (optionally emitting a Feedback toast on completion).
  • sticky: The entry stays until explicitly dismissed. The default for error entries, so a task that failed while the user was on another view is still reviewable and retryable when they return.

Navigation Guard

Whether an in-flight task arms the close / route-leave warning.

  • none: No prompt; the task continues silently in the background and the user leaves freely.
  • warn: While any task is active, leaving the app fires the beforeunload prompt and an in-app route change shows a confirm dialog. Cleared automatically when the last in-flight task resolves.

Note: the “warn before you leave with work still in flight” behavior is the same paradigm as “warn before discarding unsaved form edits.” That cross-cutting guard is tracked for harvest as its own composable intent; this intent will compose it rather than redefine it.

Composition (cross-reference, do not duplicate)

  • Loader: the upstream source. A task arrives here via Loader's escalation: async. Per-task progress reuses Loader's determinate/indeterminate indicator, version/generation token, and lifecycle states — it is the same state machine, hosted off-view.
  • Reliability: owns timeout and failure policy. Retry of an individual failed task delegates here; this surface only exposes the affordance.
  • Layout: the surface is an app-shell region (a dock). Placement and region mechanics defer to the Layout Intent.
  • Feedback: downstream. A completed task may emit a transient toast; Feedback does not track ongoing state, this intent does.

Lifecycle

A task entry in the surface moves through:

  • registered: A task has been handed off (escalated async) and now has an entry.
  • active: Work in progress; arms the navigation guard and shows progress.
  • success: Resolved; entry behaves per persistence.
  • error: Failed; entry stays (sticky) and exposes retry (via Reliability).
  • dismissed: Removed from the surface by the user or by auto-clear.

Interface Protocol

type TaskAggregation = 'single' | 'batch';
type TaskPersistence = 'transient' | 'sticky';
type TaskNavigationGuard = 'none' | 'warn';
type BackgroundTaskState = 'registered' | 'active' | 'success' | 'error' | 'dismissed';

interface BackgroundTaskIntent {
    // How concurrent tasks present in the surface
    aggregation: TaskAggregation;
    // How a completed entry persists
    persistence: TaskPersistence;
    // Whether in-flight work warns on navigate-away / close
    navigationGuard: TaskNavigationGuard;
}

interface BackgroundTask {
    id: string;
    label: string;            // sentence case, -ing verb, ≤ 3 words (Loader label conventions)
    state: BackgroundTaskState;
    // Per-task progress mirrors the Loader Intent
    progress: 'determinate' | 'indeterminate';
    value?: number;           // 0–100 when determinate
    retry?: () => void;       // present on error; delegates to Reliability
}
